other_observers: several. I don't remember all of them Bill Maynard possibly, Tony Leukering possibly, Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson possibly and John Drummond possibly; I found the bird in the net, Brian Gibbons
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch bird banding station at Holmes GRove
description: This was a very dull Vireo from the Solitary Vireo group. The bright yellow sides and flanks that faded around to the vent was too bright for CAVI. What led me to believe BHVI was the clean white throat with good clean contrast with the slaty-blue head. The head to back contrast was not very striking as it should be for BHVI. This was the only mark that was not solid for BHVI. The relatively bright green back color was good for BHVI. Another mark that was good for BHVI was the outer web of the outer rectrix (r6). This area was completely white and very wide, wider than CAVI would show.Behaviors: none noted as bird was in handCall: vireo-like fussing in handPlumage: dull female definitive
photographer: Photographer: Brian Gibbons, Brian Gibbons, Brian Gibbons
